problem_frame: The MVP needs enough structure to feel like interview practice while staying lightweight enough to validate demand quickly.

selected_approach.title: Web-first MVP with a serverless feedback pipeline
selected_approach.summary: Use a browser-based session flow with structured question state and a lightweight serverless backend that evaluates completed sessions.
pros: Fastest validation path for a solo or small team
Supports saved history and feedback reports cleanly
Keeps the architecture open for later mobile clients
cons: Less immersive than a live or voice-first experience

alternatives.title: Web-first MVP with a serverless feedback pipeline
alternatives.summary: Use a browser-based session flow with structured question state and a lightweight serverless backend that evaluates completed sessions.
pros: Fastest validation path for a solo or small team
Supports saved history and feedback reports cleanly

alternatives.title: Chat-only prototype
alternatives.summary: Use a single conversational surface with minimal session structure and looser feedback output.
cons: Harder to compare feedback across sessions

alternatives.title: Native mobile-first prototype
alternatives.summary: Build the first version as a mobile app to emphasize daily practice and notifications.
cons: Too much product and platform cost for first validation

tradeoffs: The selected approach trades immersion for faster validation and stronger structure.
The chat-only alternative is faster but makes it harder to measure rubric quality consistently.
The mobile-first path may be valuable later, but it is premature for initial demand testing.
key_decisions: Use a browser-based session flow rather than a live or native-first experience.
Model the session as structured questions with saved results rather than a free-form conversation transcript.
Generate feedback after session completion rather than during every answer.
assumptions: Typed answers are enough for the first validation cycle.
Users value structured feedback over full interview realism in an MVP.

risks.description: Feedback may feel generic if the rubric model is too weak.
risks.mitigation: Constrain the rubric output format and validate with example sessions.

risks.description: Users may abandon sessions if the setup flow is too long.
risks.mitigation: Keep the first session template short and reduce setup fields.

validation_plan: Manually test the full session flow from setup to saved report.
Use sample sessions to evaluate rubric consistency.
Check whether users finish the first full interview without guidance.
